properties.name: Village-Wellfare
properties.description: A time-management game where the goal is to help the village survive the drought.

parameters: "[\"Good ol' crops of corns need some good ol' splashes of water, wouldn't you say? I'll be needing {water} units!\",\"My field of potatoes are not doing well, unless you could send {water} units to us quick.\",\"Cabbages are better than lettuces, right? Anyways, both of them need {water} units of water.\",\"I will need {water} units of water for my banana plants. I'm sure this jungle of a town needs them.\",\"The rice fields are dying! Please, we need {water} units now!\"]"

"[\"Mister, can I have {water} units of water? I'm really thirsty!!\",\"Can't you guys just give {water} units of it? I'm sure you have enough already...\",\"Please give {water} units of water quick! I need help carrying them though.\",\"Who has full buckets here? I want {water} units of all of them!\",\"Is anyone here...? My mom is sick and she needs {water} units of water for drinking...\",\"Water. {water} units. Now. Please?\"]"

"[\"My chickens are dying of thirst! If this keeps up, we won't have eggs for breakfast. Send {water} units fast!\",\"I think my cows are on their last legs. We need {water} units otherwise we won't be lasting this summer.\",\"My sheep need {water} units of hydration! Especially Shaun, he's the best one in the flock.\",\"The pigs in my ranch need {water} units real quick, we need to sell the meat soon.\",\"I'm having some stable trouble, so if you could deliver {water} units fast that would be real nice.\"]"

"[\"Good day, could I order {water} units of water? Our household is running quite low.\",\"I am in dire need of {water} units of water right now, is anyone available??\",\"You guys have what I want, right? I definitely need {water} units and I definitely won't waste it. :3\",\"My flowers are withering because the weather is too hot lately... {water} units should do the trick.\",\"Yo, can I get {water} units? Mostly for drinking, maybe a bit for baths too.\"]"

changelog.breaking: - Resource bars now use "variants", allowing easy swapping of their visual aspect. You will have to make some adjustments to existing resource bars in your project. eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: PanelSpriteButton
description: A button that can be used for menus and most labelled buttons of a game. The button can be customized with a background for each state and a label. It handles user interactions and a simple condition can be used to check if it is clicked.
There are ready-to-use buttons in the asset-store [menu buttons pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=menu-buttons-menu-buttons).

changelog.breaking: - Buttons now use "variants", allowing easy swapping of their visual aspect. You will have to make some adjustments to existing buttons in your project. Follow this [migration documentation](https://wiki.gdevelop.io/gdevelop5/objects/custom-objects-prefab-template/migrate-to-variants/) to do these changes.

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: SpriteMultitouchJoystick
description: Multitouch joysticks are objects showing a joystick on the screen, useful for mobile. They work like a physical gamepad:

- Automatic "mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the virtual joystick (it works by reading the multitouch joystick state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.
There are ready-to-use joysticks in the asset-store [multitouch joysticks pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=multitouch-joysticks-multitouch-joysticks).

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: TwoChoicesDialogBoxes
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Two-choice dialog box with keyboard, gamepad, and touch support. Customizable text.
description: A dialog box showing multiple options (usually "yes" and "no") and a customizable text message.
It handles keyboard, gamepad and touch controls.
There are ready-to-use dialog boxes in the asset-store [dialog boxes pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=dialog-boxes-dialog-boxes).

eventsFunctions.description: Check if the events are running for the editor.
eventsFunctions.name: IsInGameEdition
eventsFunctions.sentence: Events are running for the editor

eventsBasedObjects.description: A dialog box showing two options.
eventsBasedObjects.name: TwoChoicesDialogBox

events.comment:
Gamepad, mouse, touch and keyboard are fully supported.
Each action from a device increment or decrement an index that is used to focus the active button, make outlines and trigger a sound effect.
To escape the dialog we can focus the button with the identifier 1 which is usually the button to cancel/deny by pressing one of the key or buttons

eventsFunctions.description: Check if the "Yes" button of the dialog was selected.

The button is clicked
Or
The button is active and a key is pressed to validate the button

The button is clicked
Or

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: Bouh
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: Gamepads
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Gamepad/controller support: buttons, sticks, triggers, vibration. Mapper behaviors for 2D/3D.
description: Add support for gamepads (or other physical controllers).

- automatic "gamepad mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the gamepad (it works by reading the gamepad state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.
The Bomberman-like example handles 4 players with gamepads ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://goose-bomberman)).

inlineCode: *  Associate controller button ids to button names
// Async tasks are run before everything.
// This is a hack to make sure that button states are updated
const frameBeginningTask = new class extends gdjs.AsyncTask {
// Async tasks are run before everything.

* Point and click (bypass obstacles with a predetermined polygonal chain)

Wheels grip
Only 2 wheels are simulated instead of 4 to simplify calculus.

eventsFunctions.name: ApplyWheelForces
eventsFunctions.sentence: Apply forces on the wheel at _PARAM2_ ; _PARAM3_ and _PARAM4_° of _PARAM0_

events.comment:
When cars don't slip (WheelGripRatio = 1), there is no motion orthogonally to wheels direction.

So, we project the velocity orthogonally to the wheel to find the velocity to fight:
Velocity * sin(VelocityAngle - WheelAngle)

Now, we have the velocity that we want to fight, but we need to apply a force to the object.
So, we need find how much force must be applied to have the velocity change we want.

Considering that:
- there are only 1 wheel at the front and 1 wheel at the back
- the wheels are considered to be in the same direction as the car (even if the front one car actually turn)

We have:
AngularAcceleration = Force * Distance / Inertia
Acceleration = AngularAcceleration * Distance
VelocityDelta = Acceleration * TimeDelta

Which results to:
VelocityDelta = Force / Inertia * Distance * TimeDelta

We find the force to apply to fight the velocity:
Force = VelocityDelta * Inertia / (Distance * TimeDelta)

Finally, there is a division by 2 because the car has 2 wheels and they probably fight the same velocity.
To check the equilibrium, you can set the WheelGripRatio around 2. The velocity will go back and forth and either converge (<2) or diverge (>2).

parameters: -WheelGripRatio * Object.Behavior::LinearVelocityAt(WheelX, WheelY) * sin(ToRad(Object.Behavior::LinearVelocityAngleAt(WheelX, WheelY) - WheelAngle)) * Object.Behavior::Inertia() / (DistanceSquared * TimeDelta()) / 2
